title: branch — permissions
scope_suffix_convention: Same suffix convention as user/access — `.any` / `.tenant` / `.branch` / `.own` — resolved by the `ScopeSuffixResolver` shipped by the user module. The base permission (no suffix) is the highest-tier variant, effectively `.tenant`.

permissions.name: branches.viewAny
permissions.description: Base variant — tenant-scoped list across every branch under the caller's active tenant.

permissions.name: branches.viewAny.own
permissions.description: Members see only the branches they participate in (via TenantMember / TeamMember / AthleteEnrollment).

permissions.name: branches.view
permissions.description: Read a single Branch. Every tenant member can read the branches they have visibility on.

permissions.name: branches.update_hours
permissions.description: Fine-grained permission for opening-hours edits. Separated because staff-tier roles may need to update hours without being able to touch address / capacity.

permissions.name: branches.update_hours.branch
permissions.description: Reception role can update hours only for the branches they're assigned to.

permissions.name: branches.update_address
permissions.description: Address edits are audit-sensitive — kept as a distinct permission.

permissions.name: branches.update_coordinates
permissions.description: Manual coordinate pin drops. Distinct from `update_address` because pinning without editing the address is a common map-view flow.

permissions.name: branches.update_capacity
permissions.description: Fire-safety cap edits. Compliance-relevant — kept as a distinct permission for audit clarity.

permissions.name: branches.close
permissions.description: Higher bar than update — closure affects downstream operations. Owner-tier by default.

permissions.name: branches.geocode
permissions.description: Explicitly trigger re-geocoding via `POST /branches/{branch}/geocode`. Also gated by the `branch_geocoding` entitlement.

permissions.name: branches.audit_address
permissions.description: Read the address-audit CLI-style report — validates the structured address against the country's postal format + reports drift from the geocoded canonical form.

permissions.name: platform.branches.force_close
permissions.description: Compliance-forced closure that bypasses the dependency-check kill switch. Super-admin only — every use fires an alert.

permissions.name: platform.branches.hard_delete
permissions.description: Two-person approval via `branch:hard-delete --platform-support`. Never exposed on any HTTP surface — CLI-only.
